name: I don\'t have my papers, but I was hurt at a warehouse in Salt Lake City. Can I still get help or will I get in trouble?

mainEntity.name: I don\'t have my papers, but I was hurt at a warehouse in Salt Lake City. Can I still get help or will I get in trouble?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you are eligible for workers\' comp regardless of immigration status. It is illegal for employers to deny benefits or threaten you based on your status following a workplace injury.
acceptedAnswer.description:
No. In the United States, including Utah, Idaho, and Texas, you are entitled to workers\' compensation benefits regardless of your immigration status. The law focuses on the fact that you were an employee who was injured while working. Your employer and their insurance carrier cannot use your status as a reason to deny you medical care or wage benefits.

Furthermore, it is often illegal for an employer to use the threat of deportation to stop you from filing a claim for a warehouse injury.

Your protections:
- Eligibility for medical treatment and surgery.
- Right to wage replacement if you cannot work.
- Confidentiality in many legal proceedings.

Do not let a "threat" about your status stop you from getting the medical care you need. Your health is the priority, and the law is on your side regarding the injury itself.

We have Spanish-speaking attorneys (like Mark J. Sanchez) who specialize in protecting the rights of all workers, regardless of their background or status.
